debug_info.goal:
f : ℝ → ℝ
s : Set ℝ
hs : MeasurableSet s
⊢ ∫ (x : ℝ) in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ (y : ℝ) in exp '' s, f y

have h₁ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    have h₂ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the change of variables theorem to transform the integral
    have h₃ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Define the function g(x) = exp(x)
      have h₄ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
      rw [h₄]
      -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
      have h₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Prove that exp is measurable and its inverse is measurable
        have h₆ : MeasurableSet (exp '' s) := by
          apply MeasurableSet.image
          exact hs
          exact measurable_exp.comp measurable_id
        -- Use the change of variables formula
        have h₇ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          have h₈ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
          rw [h₈]
          -- Use the fact that exp is a bijection on ℝ
          have h₉ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
            -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
            have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
            rw [h₁₀]
            -- Use the change of variables theorem
            have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
              calc
                ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                  -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
                  have h₁₂ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                    -- Use the change of variables theorem
                    have h₁₃ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                      -- Prove that exp is measurable and its inverse is measurable
                      have h₁₄ : MeasurableSet (exp '' s) := by
                        apply MeasurableSet.image
                        exact hs
                        exact measurable_exp.comp measurable_id
                      -- Use the change of variables formula
                      have h₁₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
                        rw [← Integral.comp_exp (measurableOn_id.mul (measurableOn_const : MeasurableOn (fun _ : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) _))]
                        <;>
                        simp [hs, h₁₄, Real.exp_ne_zero]
                        <;>
                        congr 1 <;>
                        ext y <;>
                        simp [Real.exp_log]
                        <;>
                        (try { aesop })
                        <;>
                        (try { field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero] })
                        <;>
                        (try { ring_nf })
                        <;>
                        (try { norm_num })
                        <;>
                        (try { linarith })
                      exact h₁₅
                    exact h₁₃
                  exact h₁₂
            exact h₁₁
          exact h₉
        exact h₇
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

              -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals

                -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                calc
                  ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by
                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                    have h8 : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by
                      -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals

                        MeasureTheory.integral_comp_mul_left (fun x => f x) (by positivity)

                      <;>
                      field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                    exact h8
                  _ = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by rfl
              exact h7
            exact h6
          _ = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by rfl
      exact h5
    exact h3
  -- Use the fact that the integrals are equal to conclude the proof
  have h6 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    linarith
  exact h6

    congr

      -- Use the change of variables theorem

          simp [mul_comm]
          <;>
          congr <;> ext <;> ring
        rw [h7]

          -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables theorem

            have h10 : ∫ (x : ℝ) in s, (fun x : ℝ => f (exp x)) x * exp x = ∫ (x : ℝ) in s, (fun x : ℝ => f (exp x)) x * exp x := rfl
            rw [h10]
            -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables theorem
            have h11 : ∫ (x : ℝ) in s, (fun x : ℝ => f (exp x)) x * exp x = ∫ (y : ℝ) in exp '' s, f y := by

                    have h15 : MeasurableSet (exp '' s) := by
                      -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the image of a measurable set under a measurable function is measurable
                      have h16 : MeasurableSet (exp '' s) := by
                        -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the image of a measurable set under a measurable function is measurable
                        apply MeasurableSet.image
                        · exact hs

                      exact h16
                    exact h15
                  -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function

                        _ = ∫ (y : ℝ) in exp '' s, f y := by rfl

have h₁ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := by simp
  have h₂ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := rfl
  rw [h₁]
  -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
  have h₃ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y :=
    by
    -- Define the exponential function and its properties
    have h₄ : (fun x : ℝ => exp x) = (fun x : ℝ => exp x) := rfl
    rw [h₄]
    -- Apply the change of variables theorem
    have h₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
    rw [h₅]
    -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the set s is measurable
    have h₆ : MeasurableSet s := hs
    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
    have h₇ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y :=
      by
        -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the set s is measurable
        have h₈ : (fun x : ℝ => exp x) = (fun x : ℝ => exp x) := rfl
        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
        have h₉ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y :=
          by
            -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the set s is measurable
            have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
            -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
            have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y :=
              by
                -- Define the function and its properties
                have h₁₂ : ∀ x : ℝ, HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => exp x) (exp x) x := by
                  intro x
                  exact hasDerivAt_exp x
                -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                have h₁₃ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y :=
                  by
                    -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the set s is measurable
                    have h₁₄ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                    calc
                      ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
                      _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                        have h₁₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                          -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the set s is measurable
                          have h₁₆ : MeasurableSet s := hs
                          -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                          have h₁₇ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                            -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                            calc
                              ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
                              _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                have h₁₈ : (fun x : ℝ => exp x) = (fun x : ℝ => exp x) := rfl
                                -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                have h₁₉ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                  -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                  have h₂₀ : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                    simp [MeasureTheory.set_integral_comp_mul_exp]
                                    <;>
                                    aesop
                                  exact h₂₀
                                exact h₁₉
                          exact h₁₇
                        exact h₁₅
                  exact h₁₃
                exact h₁₁
              exact h₁₁
            exact h₁₁
          exact h₉
        exact h₉
      exact h₇
    exact h₇
  rw [h₃]

have h₁ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
    apply integral_congr_ae
    filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem hs] with x hx
    <;> ring

  have h₂ : ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by

        have h₆ : ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := rfl
        rw [h₆]
        -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and apply the change of variables
        have h₇ : ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by

          have h₈ : Measurable (fun x : ℝ => exp x) := by continuity

              <;> aesop
            · intro x hx
              simp only [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_image]
              <;> use x
              <;> simp [hx]
              <;> aesop
          -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and apply the change of variables
          calc
            ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := rfl
            _ = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := rfl
            _ = ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x := rfl
            _ = ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x := rfl
            _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x = ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x := rfl
                rw [h₁₁]
                -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and apply the change of variables
                have h₁₂ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x = ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x := rfl
                rw [h₁₂]
                -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                have h₁₃ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => f (exp x) * exp x) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                  -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                  rw [← integral_comp_mul_left (fun x => f x)]
                  <;>
                  (try simp_all [Set.image_comp, Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]) <;>
                  (try ring_nf) <;>
                  (try simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]) <;>
                  (try norm_num) <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                      <;>
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      norm_num
                      <;>
                      linarith
                    }) <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                      <;>
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      norm_num
                      <;>
                      linarith
                    })
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                      <;>
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      norm_num
                      <;>
                      linarith
                    })
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                      <;>
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      norm_num
                      <;>
                      linarith
                    })
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      ring_nf
                      <;>
                      simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                      <;>
                      norm_num
                      <;>
                      linarith
                    })
                rw [h₁₃]
              rw [h₁₀]
          <;>
          simp_all [h₉]
          <;>
          aesop
        rw [h₇]
      rw [h₅]
    rw [h₃]
  rw [h₂]

have h₁ : (∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x)) = (∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x) := rfl
  have h₂ : (∫ y in exp '' s, f y) = (∫ y in exp '' s, (fun y => f y) y) := rfl
  have h₃ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, (fun y => f y) y := by
    -- Use the change of variables theorem to transform the integral over `s` to an integral over `exp '' s`
    have h₄ : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
    have h₅ : ∫ y in exp '' s, (fun y => f y) y = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := rfl
    -- Apply the substitution `y = exp x`
    have h₆ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
      have h₇ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
        -- The function `exp` is always positive, so we don't need to worry about the sign of the derivative
        calc
          ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by rfl
          _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
            -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
            have h₈ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
              have h₉ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                -- Use the fact that `exp` is a bijection and its derivative is always positive
                calc
                  ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by rfl
                  _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                    -- Apply the substitution `y = exp x`
                    have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                      -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
                      have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                        -- The function `exp` is always positive, so we don't need to worry about the sign of the derivative
                        have h₁₂ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                          -- Use the substitution `y = exp x`
                          have h₁₃ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                            -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
                            -- The function `exp` is always positive, so we don't need to worry about the sign of the derivative
                            have h₁₄ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                              -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
                              -- The function `exp` is a bijection and its derivative is always positive
                              have h₁₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                -- Use the substitution rule for integrals with the function `exp`
                                -- The function `exp` is always positive, so we don't need to worry about the sign of the derivative
                                rw [← MeasureTheory.set_integral_comp_mul_right (fun x => f (x)) (by positivity)]
                                <;> simp [Real.exp_ne_zero, mul_comm]
                                <;> congr 1 <;> ext y <;> simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                                <;> field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                                <;> ring_nf
                                <;> simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                                <;> norm_num
                              exact h₁₅
                            exact h₁₄
                          exact h₁₃
                        exact h₁₂
                      exact h₁₁
                    exact h₁₀
              exact h₉
            exact h₈
          _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by rfl
      exact h₇
    -- Combine the results to get the final equality
    calc
      ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by simp [h₁]
      _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by rw [h₆]
      _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, (fun y => f y) y := by simp [h₂]
  calc
    ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x := by simp [h₁]
    _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, (fun y => f y) y := by rw [h₃]
    _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by simp [h₂]

have h1 : (∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x)) = ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) := by
    simp [mul_comm]
    <;>
    congr
    <;>
    ext
    <;>
    ring
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    have h3 : ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
      have h4 : ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
        have h5 : ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) = ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) := rfl
        rw [h5]
        -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and the substitution rule for integrals
        have h6 : ∫ x in s, (exp x • f (exp x)) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
          rw [← MeasureTheory.setIntegral_comp_mul_exp (fun x => x) (by measurable) (f := f)]
          <;> simp [exp_ne_zero]
          <;>
          congr
          <;>
          ext <;>
          simp [exp_add, exp_sub, exp_neg, exp_log]
          <;>
          field_simp [exp_ne_zero]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          norm_cast
          <;>
          simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
          <;>
          linarith
        exact h6
      exact h4
    exact h3
  rw [h2]

have h1 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by rfl
  have h2 : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by
    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
    have h3 : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by
      -- The exponential function is measurable and injective
      have h4 : MeasurableSet (exp '' s) := (measurable_exp.comp hs.measurableSet)
      -- Use the change of variables formula for the exponential function
      calc
        ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := rfl
        _ = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by
          -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
          have h5 : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by
            -- Use the fact that the exponential function is injective and measurable
            have h6 : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by
              -- Use the change of variables theorem
              rw [← MeasureTheory.integral_comp_exp]
              <;> simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
              <;>
              (try norm_num) <;>
              (try simp_all [exp_ne_zero]) <;>
              (try aesop)
            exact h6
          exact h5
    exact h3
  -- Combine the results to get the final statement
  linarith

have h1 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := by
    congr
  rw [h1]
  -- Use the substitution theorem to simplify the integral
  have h2 : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
    have h3 : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
      have h4 : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
      rw [h4]
      -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
      have h5 : ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
        have h6 : AreMeasurableOn (fun x : ℝ => (exp x : ℝ)) s :=
          Measurable.exp (measurable_id) |>.measurableOn
        -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
        have h7 : (∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x)) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          -- Use the substitution y = exp(x)
          calc
            (∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x)) = ∫ x in s, (exp x : ℝ) * f (exp x) := rfl
            _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
              rw [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_exp (by
                -- Show that the substitution is valid
                exact hs
              )]
              <;>
              simp [mul_comm]
              <;>
              ring_nf
              <;>
              simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
        -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
        exact h7
      -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
      exact h5
    -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
    exact h3
  -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
  rw [h2]

have h₂ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      have h₃ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the change of variables theorem to simplify the integral
        have h₄ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          -- Use the change of variables theorem: x ↦ exp(x) is injective and measurable
          have h₅ : (fun x : ℝ => exp x) ∈ MeasurableEmbedding ℝ ℝ := by
            -- Prove that the exponential function is a measurable embedding
            refine' ⟨by continuity, _, _⟩
            · -- Prove that it is injective
              intro x y h
              -- Use the fact that exp(x) = exp(y) implies x = y
              have h₆ : x = y := by
                apply_fun (fun x => Real.log x) at h
                <;> field_simp [Real.log_exp] at h ⊢
                <;> linarith
              exact h₆
            · -- Prove that it is everywhere measurable
              intro x
              -- Since exp is continuous, it is measurable at every point
              exact measurableAt_exp
          -- Apply the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
          have h₆ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
            -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
            have h₇ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              calc
                ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
                _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                  -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
                  have h₈ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                    -- Use the fact that the exponential function is a measurable embedding
                    calc
                      ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
                      _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
                        have h₉ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                          -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
                          calc
                            ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (1 : ℝ) * (exp x * f (exp x)) := by
                              simp [mul_one]
                            _ = ∫ x in s, (1 : ℝ) * (exp x * f (exp x)) := rfl
                            _ = ∫ x in s, (1 : ℝ) * (exp x * f (exp x)) := rfl
                            _ = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by simp [mul_assoc]
                            _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                              -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
                              have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                -- Use the change of variables formula for measurable embeddings
                                calc
                                  ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                                  _ = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                                  _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for measurable embeddings
                                    have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                      -- Use the change of variables theorem for measurable embeddings
                                      calc
                                        ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                                        _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                          -- Use the change of variables theorem for measurable embeddings
                                          have h₁₂ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                            -- Use the change of variables theorem for measurable embeddings
                                            calc
                                              ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                                              _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                                -- Use the change of variables theorem for measurable embeddings
                                                rw [← MeasureTheory.integral_image_of_injective (by continuity : MeasureTheory.InjOn (fun x : ℝ => exp x) (s : Set ℝ)) (fun x y h => by
                                                  -- Prove that the exponential function is injective
                                                  have h₁₃ : exp x = exp y := by simpa using h
                                                  have h₁₄ : x = y := by
                                                    apply_fun (fun x => Real.log x) at h₁₃
                                                    <;> field_simp [Real.log_exp] at h₁₃ ⊢
                                                    <;> linarith
                                                  exact h₁₄
                                                )]
                                                <;>
                                                simp_all [Real.exp_log]
                                                <;>
                                                field_simp [Real.exp_log]
                                                <;>
                                                ring_nf
                                                <;>
                                                simp_all [Real.exp_log]
                                                <;>
                                                field_simp [Real.exp_log]
                                          exact h₁₂
                                      <;>
                                      simp_all
                                      <;>
                                      field_simp [Real.exp_log]
                                      <;>
                                      ring_nf
                                      <;>
                                      simp_all [Real.exp_log]
                                      <;>
                                      field_simp [Real.exp_log]
                                      <;>
                                      ring_nf
                                      <;>
                                      simp_all [Real.exp_log]
                                      <;>
                                      field_simp [Real.exp_log]
                                    exact h₁₁
                              exact h₁₀
                            _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by simp_all
                        exact h₉
                  exact h₈
              <;>
              simp_all
              <;>
              field_simp [Real.exp_log]
              <;>
              ring_nf
              <;>
              simp_all [Real.exp_log]
              <;>
              field_simp [Real.exp_log]
              <;>
              ring_nf
              <;>
              simp_all [Real.exp_log]
              <;>
              field_simp [Real.exp_log]
            exact h₇
          exact h₆
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
  have h₂ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by
    -- Use the change of variables formula for the exponential function
    have h₃ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by
      -- Apply the change of variables theorem
      have h₄ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
        -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
        have h₅ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
          -- Apply the change of variables formula for the exponential function
          have h₆ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
            -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
            calc
              ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := rfl
              _ = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
                -- Use the change of variables formula for the exponential function
                have h₇ : ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := by
                  -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
                  rw [MeasureTheory.integral_image]
                  <;> simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                  <;> measurability
                  <;>
                    (try
                      {
                        intros
                        <;>
                          simp_all [Real.exp_ne_zero]
                        <;>
                          norm_num
                        <;>
                          linarith
                      })
                -- Use the change of variables formula for the exponential function
                exact h₇
          -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
          exact h₆
        -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
        exact h₅
      -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
      have h₈ : ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := by
        -- Use the commutativity of multiplication
        apply integral_congr_ae
        filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem measurableSet_univ] with x hx
        ring
      -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
      calc
        ∫ y in exp '' s, f y = ∫ x in s, f (exp x) * exp x := h₄
        _ = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := h₈
    -- Use the fact that the exponential function is measurable and the change of variables formula
    exact h₃
  -- Combine the results to get the final equality
  calc
    ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
    _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by rw [h₂]
